channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Africa Needs Urgent Action to Stop $88 Billion in Illicit Financial Outflows
item.description: Oluwale Edun, Nigeria's Minister of Finance and Coordinating Minister of the Economy, don yan say Nigeria and other African countries gats take quick action to stop the $88 billion wey dey lost yearly...

item.title: Handling Misdelivered Mail: Your Legal Rights Explained
item.description: Many households experience the frustration of receiving post addressed to former occupants. According to legal expert Dean Dunham KC, residents are not obliged to act as unpaid postal intermediaries f...

item.title: Cape Town Nonprofit Inspires Foster Children Through Weekly Art Sessions
item.description: The Orange Art Project, a nonprofit based in Cape Town, is bringing creative opportunities to children living in foster care across the city. Established during the Covid-19 lockdown in 2020, the orga...

item.title: Bosnia qualify for 2026 World Cup as Italy fail again in penalty shootout
item.description: Italy don face another heartbreak as dem go miss di 2026 FIFA World Cup after Bosnia and Herzegovina knock dem out with 4-1 penalty win for Zenica. Di match start well for Italy, as Moise Kean give de...

item.title: ACT Supreme Court seeks government explanation on suitability of rehabilitation sentence for young offender
item.description: Acting Justice Patricia Kelly of the ACT Supreme Court has called on the ACT government to explain why a 20-year-old man, who pleaded guilty to sexually abusing his six- or seven-year-old niece, was d...

item.title: Sandiganbayan convicts two former DPWH officials for fraudulent ROW claims in South Cotabato
item.description: The Sandiganbayan’s Fourth Division has handed down convictions against two former officials of the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) in Soccsksargen, linking them to fraudulent payments ...

item.title: Canada and Mexico pursue distinct strategies as USMCA review approaches
item.description: With the USMCA review set for July 1, Canada and Mexico are approaching the process differently. Mexico has already started technical discussions with the U.S., focusing on vehicle content rules, supp...

item.title: Calgary Property Taxes to Rise by 8.1% Due to Provincial and Municipal Changes
item.description: Calgary homeowners will experience a significant increase in property taxes this year, primarily driven by the provincial government's increased share. City council recently finalized an overall resid...

item.title: The Real Life of Tanzanian Digital Creators: Challenges, Adaptation, and Growth
item.description: For many digital creators in Tanzania, the journey is far from the glamorous image often shown online. VICENT NJAU, popularly known as Kiredio, started his career in stand-up comedy but struggled with...

item.title: MK Stalin launches Tamil Nadu election campaign, addresses key political issues
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin has officially launched his election campaign ahead of the upcoming state assembly polls. During his campaign event, he highlighted the government's achievements in...

item.title: Canada hikes permanent residence fees for Nigerians and other foreign applicants from April 30, 2026
item.description: Canada don announce say e go increase the fees wey people dey pay to apply for permanent residence, and this one go start from April 30, 2026. Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) talk ...

item.title: Growing Concentration of Fertilizer Ships Raises Trade and Security Concerns in Persian Gulf
item.description: A growing number of fertilizer-laden ships has been gathering in the Persian Gulf, signaling potential shifts in regional trade dynamics amid heightened tensions linked to the ongoing Iran conflict. A...

item.title: Nairobi Governor Sakaja Faces Arrest Over Senate Summons Noncompliance
item.description: Police in Nairobi have launched a full-scale operation at City Hall to locate Governor Johnson Sakaja after a Senate committee ordered his arrest for contempt of Parliament. Sakaja reportedly failed t...

item.title: Mark Ronson returns to the studio after joking about his career slowdown and wife Grace Gummer’s rising fame
item.description: British musician and producer Mark Ronson, 50, has shared a self-deprecating post on Instagram joking that his career is 'drying up' while his wife, actress Grace Gummer, is becoming 'more famous' tha...

item.title: France denies Israel permission to use its airspace for transporting weapons to Iran conflict
item.description: France has taken a clear stand by refusing Israel permission to use French airspace for transporting American weapons intended for the ongoing conflict in Iran. This marks the first time France has ta...

item.title: Actress Carey Mulligan receives CBE for services to drama
item.description: British actress Carey Mulligan has been appointed a Commander of the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire (CBE) by King Charles at an investiture ceremony at Windsor Castle. Recognised for her c...

item.title: Angeline Mtulia: How a Corporate Lawyer Drives Business with Ethics and Leadership
item.description: Angeline Mtulia, a corporate lawyer at Africa Global Logistics Tanzania Limited (AGL), shares her journey of blending law with business growth. From her early interest in law to working at a top Tanza...

item.title: Kenyan Musician Paul Waiganjo Denies Running for Ol Kalou MP Seat
item.description: Several posts were going round on social media claiming that gospel musician Paul Waiganjo was running for the Ol Kalou parliamentary seat following the passing of MP David Kiaraho on 29 March 2026. T...

item.title: Kenya Beats Grenada 3-0 to Finish Third in FIFA Series Tournament
item.description: Kenya's Harambee Stars ended their FIFA Series campaign on a high note by defeating Grenada 3-0 in the third-place playoff held at Kigali's Amahoro Stadium. After a heartbreaking 5-4 penalty loss to E...

item.title: Peninsula Club Promotes Education via Diplomatic Golf Tournament Sponsorship
item.description: The Peninsula Club in Dar es Salaam has shown strong commitment to community development and youth empowerment by sponsoring the 2026 Diplomatic Golf Tournament. This event, organized by the Songea Mi...
